channel.title: Build 2026 on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Build 2026 on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: ai: Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
item.description:
Microsoft’s Build 2026 post frames AI agent production challenges around accuracy, safety, accountability, and silent drift after deployment. It points to end-to-end observability as the path to managing agents across frameworks and tying operations back to business ROI.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code (June Build 2026 refresh)
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code is now generally available with a Build 2026 refresh. The update adds the full model catalog, model playground, and Hosted agent deployment directly in the editor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: commandline: Introducing Command Line, and the new rules for builders
item.description:
Microsoft introduces a new “rules for builders” framing around agentic AI development, saying the traditional software development lifecycle is no longer sufficient. The post positions the guidance as a shift in workflows and mindset for building with AI agents.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Build 2026: Securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle
item.description:
Microsoft says Build 2026 introduced MDASH and additional security capabilities aimed at securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle. The post frames these updates as support for fast AI development with security controls built in.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | Build Edition
item.description:
Microsoft Build 2026 introduces several Microsoft Foundry updates for agent developers, including hosted runtimes, Toolboxes, memory, Voice Live, Foundry IQ, new models, managed compute, and added trust, evaluation, and observability features.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog
